name: Hades (Pluto in Roman mythology)
shortDescription: Hades, known as Pluto in Roman mythology, was the god of the Underworld, ruling over the dead. He was a feared, but not necessarily evil, figure in Greek and Roman belief.
description: Hades, the Greek god of the Underworld, also known as Pluto in Roman mythology, was one of the three sons of Cronus and Rhea, and brother to Zeus and Poseidon. After the defeat of the Titans, the three brothers divided the cosmos, with Zeus taking the sky, Poseidon the sea, and Hades the Underworld. Hades ruled over the realm of the dead, a dark and shadowy place often depicted as being guarded by Cerberus, the multi-headed dog. He was not considered an Olympian, as he resided permanently in the Underworld. While feared for his association with death, Hades was not inherently evil; he maintained order in his realm and ensured the dead remained there. His most famous myth involves the abduction of Persephone, daughter of Demeter, whom he took as his wife and queen of the Underworld. This act caused Demeter's grief to blight the earth, leading to the creation of the seasons. Hades is often depicted with a helmet that renders him invisible, a gift from the Cyclopes. He is also associated with wealth, as the earth was believed to hold precious metals and gems.
